questions.text: Which blood cells are responsible for fighting against germs that enter our body?
questions.explanation: WBCs act as the defense system of the body by attacking pathogens.

questions.text: The fluid part of the blood is called:
questions.explanation: Plasma is a straw-colored liquid that carries cells, nutrients, and waste products.

questions.text: Which blood vessels carry oxygen-rich blood from the heart to all parts of the body?
questions.explanation: Arteries have thick elastic walls to withstand the high pressure of blood from the heart.

questions.text: The rhythmic contraction and expansion of the heart is called:
questions.explanation: A heartbeat consists of one systole and one diastole.

questions.text: The main excretory product in humans is:
questions.explanation: Humans are ureotelic, meaning they primarily excrete nitrogenous waste as urea.

questions.text: Which tissue is responsible for the transport of water and nutrients in plants?
questions.explanation: Xylem forms a continuous network of channels that connects roots to the leaves.

questions.text: The process of evaporation of water through stomata is called:
questions.explanation: Transpiration creates a suction pull that helps pull water to great heights in tall trees.

questions.text: Which organ filters the blood to remove wastes as urine?
questions.explanation: Kidneys contain millions of tiny filtering units called nephrons.

questions.text: The red pigment in RBCs that binds with oxygen is:
questions.explanation: Hemoglobin gives blood its red color and efficiently transports oxygen.

questions.text: Phloem is responsible for the transport of ________ in plants.
questions.explanation: Phloem transports sugars prepared in the leaves to all other parts of the plant.
